title: Bytes, Object Representation, Alignment, Padding, and Endianness · Guided Pointer Studio
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working portable binary representation codec while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of portable binary representation codec while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.
Compute and satisfy fundamental, extended, member, and allocated alignment requirements before access.
Measure rather than assume member offsets, tail padding, and layout changes across ABI targets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Byte Object Representation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 8358. Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.

What must be established before implementation?

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Explicit Clearing condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Explicit Clearing case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Explicit Clearing
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Explicit Clearing: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 5070. Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use C23 memset_explicit or a reviewed non-elidable alternative for secrets and verify compiler and library support.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Explicit Clearing.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Explicit Clearing, keep Explicit Clearing connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use C23 memset_explicit or a reviewed non-elidable alternative for secrets and verify compiler and library support.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Explicit Clearing, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Explicit Clearing precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Explicit Clearing, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 5070. Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use C23 memset_explicit or a reviewed non-elidable alternative for secrets and verify compiler and library support.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Explicit Clearing, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: Ordinary memset is guaranteed to remain when the cleared object is no longer read.
